moduleDescription:
This module contains helpers that deal with different byte orders (endian).

Endianness is the order of bytes of a value in memory. Big-endian means that the most significant byte is stored at the smallest memory address, while little endian means that the least-significant byte is stored at the smallest address. See also https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Endianness.

Unstable API.

entries.name: swapEndian64
entries.code:
proc swapEndian64(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, noSideEffect, raises: [],
                                        tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, reversing the byte order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 8 bytes.

entries.name: swapEndian32
entries.code:
proc swapEndian32(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, noSideEffect, raises: [],
                                        tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, reversing the byte order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 4 bytes.

entries.name: swapEndian16
entries.code:
proc swapEndian16(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, noSideEffect, raises: [],
                                        tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, reversing the byte order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 2 bytes.

entries.name: littleEndian64
entries.code:
proc littleEndian64(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, storing it in 64-bit little-endian order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 8 bytes.

entries.name: littleEndian32
entries.code:
proc littleEndian32(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, storing it in 32-bit little-endian order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 4 bytes.

entries.name: littleEndian16
entries.code:
proc littleEndian16(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [],
    forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, storing it in 16-bit little-endian order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 2 bytes.

entries.name: bigEndian64
entries.code: proc bigEndian64(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, storing it in 64-bit big-endian order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 8 bytes.

entries.name: bigEndian32
entries.code: proc bigEndian32(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, storing it in 32-bit big-endian order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 4 bytes.

entries.name: bigEndian16
entries.code: proc bigEndian16(outp, inp: pointer) {.inline, raises: [], tags: [], forbids: [].}
entries.description: Copies inp to outp, storing it in 16-bit big-endian order. Both buffers are supposed to contain at least 2 bytes.
